title: Pokopia Dive Update: How to Unlock Dive and Explore Underwater
seo_description: Official Pokopia Dive update details: release window, unlock requirements, Surf requirement, Bleak Beach request, and what underwater exploration changes.
excerpt: The free Pokopia Dive update is scheduled for August 2026 and lets Ditto explore underwater after clearing specific Bleak Beach and Surf requirements.
content:
The official Pokopia update page confirms that a free Dive update is planned for August 2026. The update adds underwater exploration through the Dive move, letting Ditto move beneath the surface after meeting the required progression conditions.

To unlock Dive, players need to complete the Raise the environment level! request in Bleak Beach. The official page also says the player must already be able to jump and must know Surf before Dive becomes available. That makes the update relevant for players planning route progression, water-area unlocks, and late early-game preparation.

This is a free update for players who own Pokemon Pokopia. It is separate from the paid Expansion Pass, so players should not confuse the Dive feature with the Bubbly Basin DLC content. We recommend checking Bleak Beach progression, Surf access, and water-type team coverage before the August update window.
image_alt: Pokémon Pokopia official promotional screenshot used for Dive update coverage
image_source: Gematsu media page - third-party repost of official Pokopia promotional screenshot
image_license_note: Screenshot is used for editorial identification and commentary with source attribution. Official update facts are sourced from the linked Pokemon Pokopia update page.

source_review_notes: Primary source: Official Pokemon Pokopia Update Page.
Source type is recorded as Official and should be checked before this article is treated as current.
This article summarizes the linked source and should link readers to official or clearly labeled context for exact wording.
claim_limits: Do not treat the August 2026 update window as a precise launch date unless the official update page states one.
Do not merge the free Dive update with paid Bubbly Basin Expansion Pass content.
Do not add underwater spawn, reward, or route details until an official source or reviewed testing supports them.
recheck_triggers: The official update page changes the release window, Dive unlock requirements, Surf requirement, or Bleak Beach request wording.
Nintendo or Pokemon publishes launch notes for the Dive update.
A guide cites this page for underwater route, spawn, or progression requirements.
confirmed_facts: The official Pokémon Pokopia update page lists the underwater exploration update for August 2026.
The update is available to everyone who owns Pokémon Pokopia.
Dive lets Ditto move underwater.
Learning Dive requires the “Raise the environment level!” request in Bleak Beach, the ability to jump, and Surf.
reader_takeaways: Treat August 2026 as a release window, not a confirmed day, until the official page provides one.
Keep the free Dive update separate from the paid Bubbly Basin Expansion Pass when planning a purchase.
Do not rely on this page for underwater spawn, reward, or route details until an official source or documented testing supports them.
data_status_note: This is a reviewed official-source update. Confirmed facts are limited to the linked Pokémon page; route advice and future underwater details remain outside its scope.

title: Pokopia Expansion Pass Bubbly Basin: Release Window, Bonuses, and Requirements
seo_description: Official Pokopia Expansion Pass details for Bubbly Basin, including the August 2026 release window, purchase bonuses, rare Pokemetal ingots, and what is included.
excerpt: Pokopia's Expansion Pass Part 1, Bubbly Basin, is planned for August 2026 with an underwater town, new items, new Pokemon friends, and early purchase bonuses.
content:
The official Pokemon Pokopia Expansion Pass page confirms that Part 1, Bubbly Basin, is planned for August 2026. Bubbly Basin is described as an underwater town, with new furniture, new clothing items, and more Pokemon friends tied to the paid DLC.

The Expansion Pass is separate from the free Dive update. The official page lists purchase bonuses that include Ditto-patterned block and wallpaper recipes. It also says players who purchase by August 31, 2027 can receive a code for 30 rare Pokemetal ingots, subject to the official terms and regional availability.

Players comparing the base game, free update, and DLC should treat Bubbly Basin as paid expansion content, not a standard patch feature. If you are planning around the August 2026 window, the practical checklist is to confirm eShop availability in your region, separate the Expansion Pass from hardware bundles, and prepare water-area progression before the DLC content arrives.
source_label: Official Pokemon Pokopia Expansion Pass Page
image_alt: Pokémon Pokopia official promotional screenshot used for Bubbly Basin Expansion Pass coverage

confirmed_facts: Pokémon Pokopia Expansion Pass is paid DLC available through Nintendo eShop.
Part 1, Bubbly Basin, is listed for August 2026.
Starting Bubbly Basin requires the “Raise the environment level!” request in Bleak Beach and the Dive move from the free update.
The Expansion Pass includes a recipe for dynamic Ditto print blocks and wallpaper.
The official page lists Part 2 for late 2026 and Part 3 for 2027.
reader_takeaways: Free Dive access is a prerequisite for this paid DLC, but the update itself is not DLC.
Check your regional Nintendo eShop before treating bonus eligibility or availability as universal.
Use Nintendo’s claim instructions for bonus recipes and codes instead of relying on screenshots or reposts.
data_status_note: This is a reviewed official-source DLC summary. Dates, purchase bonuses, claim steps, and regional availability must be rechecked against Nintendo before purchase.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Wish Upon a Jirachi Event: Archived Official Details
seo_title: Pokémon Pokopia Jirachi Event: Archived Details
seo_description: Archived official details for Pokémon Pokopia’s Wish Upon a Jirachi event, including its June 23 to July 8, 2026 schedule, requirements, and limitations.
excerpt: This official Wish Upon a Jirachi event ran from June 23 through July 8, 2026. This page preserves the confirmed requirements, rewards context, and multiplayer limitations.
content:
Nintendo’s Wish Upon a Jirachi event ran from June 23 at 5:00 a.m. through July 8, 2026 at 4:59 a.m., based on the Nintendo Switch 2 system clock. The event is now archived on Pokopia Portal; this page should not be used as a current event listing.

During the event, Nintendo said players could befriend Jirachi, receive a wish note recipe, fulfill Pokémon requests, and exchange shining wish notes for star-themed furniture and other event items. It required Pokémon Pokopia Version 1.1.0 or later, an internet connection on first start, and completion of important town requests through rebuilding the Pokémon Center.

The official announcement also limited the event to towns and Palette Town. It did not occur on Cloud Islands, and Palette Town multiplayer required Spectator Mode to be off. Keep those limits attached to the archived event rather than using them as a general rule for every future event.
source_label: Nintendo Australia - Wish Upon a Jirachi Event
image_alt: Jirachi and star-themed rewards featured in the Pokémon Pokopia Wish Upon a Jirachi event
image_license_note: Image is hosted by Nintendo and used for editorial identification of the officially announced event with source attribution.
image_original_media: Nintendo official Pokémon Pokopia event artwork
source_review_notes: Primary source: Nintendo Australia - Wish Upon a Jirachi Event.
Source type is recorded as Official and should be checked before this article is treated as current.
This article summarizes the linked source and should link readers to official or clearly labeled context for exact wording.
claim_limits: Do not extend the event beyond the official start and end times.
Do not claim Cloud Island support when the source says the event does not occur on Cloud Islands.
Do not add extra rewards, request rates, or multiplayer behavior beyond the event announcement.
recheck_triggers: Nintendo updates the event announcement, version requirement, timing, rewards, or multiplayer rules.
Another region publishes different event wording.
The event ends and the page needs archived-event wording.
confirmed_facts: Nintendo listed the event from June 23 at 5:00 a.m. through July 8, 2026 at 4:59 a.m., based on the system clock.
Nintendo required Version 1.1.0 or later, internet access on first start, and progression through rebuilding the Pokémon Center.
The event did not occur on Cloud Islands.
Palette Town multiplayer participation required Spectator Mode to be off.
reader_takeaways: The scheduled period has ended, so do not plan a current route around this event without a new official announcement.
Use this page as a record of Nintendo’s event requirements and limitations, not as a prediction of a rerun.
Future event pages should be updated to archived status as soon as their official end time has passed.
data_status_note: Archived after the official July 8, 2026 end time. The article preserves source-backed historical details and does not claim that the event is currently available.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 + Pokémon Pokopia Bundle Launches July 23 in Singapore
seo_description: Nintendo Singapore will launch a Nintendo Switch 2 and Pokémon Pokopia bundle on July 23, 2026. See what is included and what is not.
excerpt: Nintendo Singapore has announced a regional Nintendo Switch 2 + Pokémon Pokopia bundle for July 23, 2026, including a full-game download.
content:
Nintendo Singapore has announced that a Nintendo Switch 2 + Pokémon Pokopia bundle will launch in Singapore on July 23, 2026. This is a regional announcement; pricing, stock, and availability may differ in other markets.

The package includes a Nintendo Switch 2 console, dock, Joy-Con 2 controllers, two Joy-Con 2 straps, a Joy-Con 2 grip, AC adapter, USB-C charging cable, Ultra High Speed HDMI cable, and a full-game download of Pokémon Pokopia. An internet connection and Nintendo Account are required to download the game.

Nintendo states that the Pokémon Pokopia Expansion Pass is not included with the hardware bundle. A separate Pokémon Pokopia + Pokémon Pokopia Expansion Pass software bundle is available through Nintendo eShop. Buyers should check their local Nintendo store before treating the Singapore release date or package details as applicable to another region.
source_label: Nintendo Singapore - Switch 2 + Pokémon Pokopia Bundle
image_alt: Nintendo Switch 2 and Pokémon Pokopia bundle announced for Singapore
image_license_note: Image is hosted by Nintendo and used for editorial identification of the officially announced regional product bundle with source attribution.

Nintendo says the Expansion Pass is not included with the hardware bundle.
reader_takeaways: This is a Singapore-specific announcement; do not apply its date, stock, price, or contents to another region.
Check whether a local retailer is selling a hardware bundle or a separate software-and-DLC bundle before buying.
Use the source link for the current package list because hardware offers can change without affecting game content.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Release Date and Platform Confirmed by Nintendo
excerpt: Nintendo lists Pokémon Pokopia for Nintendo Switch 2 with a March 5, 2026 release date, US pricing, file size, supported languages, and early purchase bonus details.
content:
Nintendo's product page lists Pokémon Pokopia as a Nintendo Switch 2 title scheduled for March 5, 2026 in the United States. The same listing includes the US price, file size, supported languages, play modes, and Nintendo Switch Online-related features.

For readers, this page should be treated as a source roundup rather than a rumor post. We will update it only when Nintendo or The Pokémon Company publishes newer product information.

The listing also describes an early purchase bonus: an in-game Ditto Rug available through Mystery Gift for eligible players. Check the official product page for exact claim timing and regional availability.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Gameplay Overview: Confirmed Building and Life Sim Features
excerpt: Official Nintendo and Pokémon pages describe Pokémon Pokopia as a cozy life simulation about rebuilding a world, crafting, growing crops, learning moves, and befriending Pokémon.
content:
Official Pokémon Pokopia materials describe the game around building, crafting, gardening, exploration, and befriending Pokémon. The player character is a Ditto transformed to look like a human, and the broad goal is to shape a desolate world into a more livable Pokémon utopia.

Nintendo describes activities such as gathering materials, crafting furniture, growing crops, cooking meals, rebuilding a town, taking photos, and customizing the player character. The official Pokémon site also highlights creating habitats and inviting Pokémon or friends to visit.

This article intentionally sticks to confirmed systems. Route advice, habitat recommendations, and database entries elsewhere on Pokopia Portal are editorial guides and should be read with their content status notes.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Multiplayer and GameShare Details Explained
excerpt: Nintendo explains town visits, Spectator Mode, Palette Town, GameShare, Cloud Island, and Virtual Mode for Pokémon Pokopia multiplayer.
content:
Nintendo has published a dedicated explanation of Pokémon Pokopia multiplayer. The article separates normal town visits, Palette Town co-building, GameShare access, Cloud Island, and Virtual Mode.

A key detail is that normal visitors enter a town in Spectator Mode, which limits what they can change. Palette Town is presented as the larger shared-building space where players can work together more freely.

For site guides, this matters because multiplayer should not be described as one generic mode. Future community and build articles should distinguish visiting, co-building, GameShare, Cloud Island, and Virtual Mode when discussing multiplayer.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Beginner Tips from Nintendo’s Official Guide
excerpt: Nintendo’s official tips emphasize pacing, Pokémon Center PC features, bag upgrades, Pokémon requests, storage boxes, Pokédex filters, and food-powered moves.
content:
Nintendo’s official tips article frames Pokémon Pokopia as a game players can approach at their own pace. It recommends checking Pokémon Center PC features, improving storage, talking to Pokémon, organizing materials, and using the Pokédex and Habitat Dex filters.

The tips also mention that food can power up Ditto’s moves. That official context is why Pokopia Portal recipe pages now link back to official beginner information before giving editorial recommendations.

This article is a source summary, not a replacement for the full Nintendo guide. Use the official article for exact wording and current tips, then use our guides for route-specific editorial planning.

Pokopia Portal uses screenshots with visible source attribution because Pokémon Pokopia is a visual game and readers need to inspect actual gameplay material. Official Pokémon pages note that shown footage and screenshots may come from a product in development and may not represent final gameplay.

When we use third-party pages that repost official screenshots, the image caption should identify the source clearly. Long term, the preferred setup is to use official press-kit assets or self-captured screenshots hosted locally.

This article exists to keep image sourcing transparent. It should not be treated as a new trailer announcement unless a linked official trailer page says so.

title: What Is Pokémon Pokopia? Official Premise and Gameplay Summary
excerpt: Pokémon Pokopia follows a human-like Ditto rebuilding a world for Pokémon through crafting, moves, materials, habitats, and cozy life-sim activities.
content:
Official materials describe Pokémon Pokopia as a relaxing life-simulation and creation game rather than a traditional battle-first Pokémon RPG. The player controls a Ditto that has transformed to resemble a human, then explores and rebuilds a world for Pokémon.

The confirmed loop includes gathering materials, learning moves from Pokémon, crafting furniture, growing crops, and shaping spaces where Pokémon can live. Professor Tangrowth is described by Nintendo as a guide for the player and the Pokémon they meet.

This summary is meant for readers who are new to the game. For deeper route planning, use the guide and wiki pages, but keep their editorial status in mind.
source_label: Nintendo News - Shape the World in Pokémon Pokopia

content:
Nintendo’s product page lists Pokémon Pokopia as a Nintendo Switch 2 game with TV mode, tabletop mode, handheld mode, online play, local wireless support, GameShare, and save data cloud support. Some features require Nintendo Switch Online or Nintendo Switch Online + Expansion Pack.

This distinction matters for readers comparing solo, local, and online play. We recommend checking Nintendo’s product page for current requirements before buying, because subscription and feature availability can vary by region or account setup.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Confirmed Moves: Leafage, Surf, and Glide
excerpt: Nintendo describes Pokémon moves such as Bulbasaur’s Leafage, Lapras’s Surf, and Dragonite’s Glide as tools that change exploration and world-building options.
content:
Nintendo’s Pokémon Pokopia product information gives examples of moves that affect exploration and creation. Bulbasaur’s Leafage, Lapras’s Surf, and Dragonite’s Glide are specifically described as moves that can help the player shape or move through the world.

This is important because moves in Pokémon Pokopia are not only battle actions. Official descriptions connect them to traversal, building, and environmental interaction.

Guide pages that recommend routes or habitats should avoid inventing confirmed move behavior unless it is sourced. When a move detail is editorial or database-based, the page should label it accordingly.

Route pages should link to this update when they need confirmed move context, then separate any personal testing from official facts.
data_status_note: This is a reviewed official-source system summary. It intentionally stays narrow: confirmed move examples are separated from unverified move lists, balance claims, and route advice.

title: Pokémon Pokopia PC, Requests, and Daily Challenges Explained
excerpt: Nintendo’s tips article points players toward Pokémon Center PC features, daily challenges, PC shop items, Pokémon requests, stamps, and Life Coins.
content:
Nintendo’s official tips recommend checking the Pokémon Center PC because it connects to daily challenges, PC shop items, recipes, and stamps that can be exchanged for Life Coins.

The same tips encourage players to talk to befriended Pokémon, especially when they seem to be thinking about something, because this can lead to requests. This gives the site a confirmed basis for future beginner guides about routine planning.

Any detailed route, resource, or reward recommendations on Pokopia Portal should still be treated as editorial unless they are directly backed by Nintendo or Pokémon source pages.

title: Pokémon Pokopia Cloud Island and Palette Town Explained
excerpt: Nintendo describes Palette Town as a shared creative space and Cloud Island as a persistent online area with its own Pokédex, rewards, and challenges.
content:
Nintendo’s multiplayer explainer separates Palette Town and Cloud Island. Palette Town is described as a large sandbox area where players can build, craft, discover Pokémon, and create habitats together when Spectator Mode is turned off.

Cloud Island is described as a persistent online area with its own Pokédex, rewards, and challenges. Nintendo also describes Virtual Mode as a way to share a Cloud Island online while preventing visitors from changing it.

For readers, the main takeaway is that multiplayer spaces have different permissions and purposes. Do not assume all visiting, co-building, and online-sharing features work the same way.
source_label: Nintendo News - How Multiplayer Works in Pokémon Pokopia

title: Pokopia Portal Site Update: How We Label Official and Editorial Content
excerpt: Pokopia Portal now separates official source roundups from editorial guides, database entries, habitat routes, and recipe recommendations.
content:
Pokopia Portal is changing how news and guide pages present source status. Official source roundup pages summarize Nintendo and Pokémon information. Guide, Pokémon, Habitat, and Recipe pages remain editorial references unless a claim is explicitly sourced.

This change is meant to make the site clearer for readers and safer for search quality. It also helps prevent editorial route planning from being mistaken for official game data.

Readers can use the Content Status and Official Source Roundup boxes near the top of pages to understand whether they are reading confirmed information, editorial analysis, or site database guidance.

title: Pokemon Pokopia Sableye Gem Hunt: Archived Official Details
seo_title: Pokemon Pokopia Sableye Gem Hunt: Archived Details
seo_description: Archived official details for Pokemon Pokopia’s Sableye Gem Hunt, including its April 29 to May 14, 2026 schedule, Dream Island route, and town-only limitation.
excerpt: Sableye’s Gem Hunt ran from April 29 through May 14, 2026 in local time. This archived page preserves Nintendo’s confirmed event route, town requirement, and Cloud Island limitation.
content:
Nintendo’s Sableye Gem Hunt ran from April 29 at 5:00 a.m. until May 14, 2026 at 4:59 a.m. local time. The event has ended, so this page is an archived record rather than a current event guide. Nintendo said that players could befriend Sableye, collect event-only red crystal fragments, and exchange them for gem-hunt-inspired furniture and other items.

The official route began by talking to Sableye near the Pokemon Center. Players then examined a doll so Drifloon could take them to a Dream Island, where red crystal fragments could be collected. The fragments could be traded at the Pokemon Center for items including building kits and furniture.

Nintendo limited the event to towns where the Pokemon Center had been repaired. It did not occur on Cloud Island. Nintendo also said that Pokemon befriended through this event remained in town after the event ended. Keep that as a historical event detail, not as a guarantee for a future rerun.
source_label: Nintendo: Join Sableye’s Gem Hunt in Pokemon Pokopia
source_review_notes: Primary source: Nintendo’s official Sableye Gem Hunt announcement.
The event schedule and route are preserved as historical details only because the official end date has passed.
claim_limits: Do not present this as a current event or infer that it will return.
Do not add drop rates, reward quantities, or Dream Island rules not stated by Nintendo.
recheck_triggers: Nintendo announces a rerun, revised schedule, or new regional event page.
Nintendo corrects the original event conditions or reward description.
confirmed_facts: Nintendo listed the event from April 29 at 5:00 a.m. through May 14, 2026 at 4:59 a.m. local time.
Nintendo said players could befriend Sableye and collect event-only red crystal fragments on a Dream Island.
Nintendo said fragments could be exchanged at the Pokemon Center for building kits, furniture, and other items.
The event required a repaired Pokemon Center in a town and did not occur on Cloud Island.
reader_takeaways: The event has ended; do not plan a current route around its materials or rewards without a new official announcement.
The archived route is useful for understanding how Nintendo scoped a town-only event and its Cloud Island restriction.
Sableye befriended during the original event was stated to remain in town after the event ended.
data_status_note: Archived after Nintendo’s May 14, 2026 end time. This source-backed record preserves confirmed historical details and does not claim that the event or its red crystal fragments are currently available.

title: Pokemon Pokopia More Spores for Hoppip: Archived Official Details
seo_title: Pokemon Pokopia Hoppip Event: Archived Details
seo_description: Archived official details for Pokemon Pokopia’s More Spores for Hoppip event, including its March 2026 schedule, Pokemon Center requirement, and Cloud Island restriction.
excerpt: More Spores for Hoppip ran from March 9 through March 24, 2026. This archived record preserves Nintendo’s confirmed cotton spore objective, repaired Pokemon Center requirement, and Cloud Island limitation.
content:
Nintendo’s More Spores for Hoppip event ran from March 9 at 1:00 p.m. PT through March 24, 2026 at 12:59 p.m. PT. The event has ended, and this page is retained as a source-backed archive rather than a current event listing. Nintendo said players could collect cotton spores during the event to become friends with Hoppip, Skiploom, and Jumpluff.

The official launch article also set two important boundaries: the event occurred only in towns where the Pokemon Center had been repaired, and it did not occur on Cloud Island. Those conditions describe this specific event and should not be generalized to every future event.

Use this page as historical context for Nintendo’s early event structure, not as a prediction of a rerun or a live reward list. A new official announcement would be needed before treating cotton spores, the friendship targets, or the time window as currently available.
source_label: Nintendo: Pokemon Pokopia available now launch article
source_review_notes: Primary source: Nintendo’s official Pokemon Pokopia launch article.
The event is archived because the source’s March 24, 2026 end time is in the past.
claim_limits: Do not claim that cotton spores, friendship targets, or rewards are currently obtainable.
Do not apply this event’s repaired-Pokemon-Center and Cloud Island conditions to all future events.
recheck_triggers: Nintendo announces a rerun, replacement event, or revised participation rules.
Nintendo updates the launch article with a correction to the event schedule or conditions.
confirmed_facts: Nintendo listed the event from March 9 at 1:00 p.m. PT through March 24, 2026 at 12:59 p.m. PT.
Nintendo said players could collect cotton spores to become friends with Hoppip, Skiploom, and Jumpluff.
Nintendo said the event occurred only in towns where the Pokemon Center had been repaired.
Nintendo said the event did not occur on Cloud Island.
reader_takeaways: The scheduled event has ended, so it should not be used as a live event recommendation.
This record clarifies the specific town and Cloud Island boundaries that applied to the Hoppip event.
A current official event announcement is required before planning around cotton spores or these friendship targets.
data_status_note: Archived after Nintendo’s March 24, 2026 end time. This source-backed record preserves the announced schedule and participation limits without claiming that the event is currently active.
